items.title: Thieves steal around €4 million in jewellery from French museum

items.title: World Cup 2026 : France battle through Paraguay dark arts
items.description: France is through to the round of 16 after a fierce battle with Paraguay. Paraguay sat in a low block and tried to frustrate les Bleus, often committing bad fouls, all of which went unpunished. Kylian Mbappé scored the only goal of the game, converting a penalty. Morocco were slightly stunned by Canada in the first half of their game in Houston, unfortunately, the Canucks were unable to keep the pressure going and conceded the first goal of the fixture just after half time. Last minute risks did not pay off for the co-hosts who suffered two more goals at the hands of the Atlas Lions. That 3-0 win means that Morocco will face France in the quarter finals, a dramatic remake of the 2022 World Cup.

items.title: Could islamist fighters take Timbuktu?
items.description: Mali's military junta is facing one of its most serious security challenges in months. A wave of coordinated attacks by the jihadist group JNIM and its Tuareg ally, the FLA, has targeted army positions across the north of the country, raising fresh questions about the government's ability to contain the insurgency.

items.title: Guam on alert as Super Typhoon Bavi closes in
items.description: Howling winds and lashing rains battered Guam and the Northern Marianas late Sunday hours before the projected arrival of a "super typhoon" with equivalent force to a category-5 hurricane over the US Pacific territories. Super Typhoon Bavi was forecast to roar westwards over the area early Monday with maximum sustained winds of up to 280 kilometres (173 miles) per hour and gusts of 333 kmh, according to the Joint Typhoon Warning Center.

items.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
items.description: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

items.title: Marine Le Pen faces key appeal ruling over 2027 presidential bid
items.description: France's far-right chief Marine Le Pen faces a key ruling next week that will determine if she can run in the 2027 presidential elections, in what is seen as her best chance at the top job. A lower court handed the 57-year-old politician a five-year ban from public office last year and a two-year sentence over a fake jobs scam at the European Parliament, dashing her ambition to head the EU state. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle brings you this analysis.

The blaze, which has already scorched 1,500 hectares near the Pyrenees, is part of a wider wave of fires across southern Europe fuelled by extreme heat, forcing evacuations and raising fears of a long, intense wildfire season.

items.title: Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger begin official withdrawal from ICC

items.title: Alibaba bans Claude for staff – Anthropic didn't want them using it anyway
items.description: Chinese tech giant Alibaba has ordered staff to stop using Anthropic's Claude Code, after it was found to be flagging users connecting from China. But Anthropic is already trying its best to stop Chinese firms from using Claude at all, and accuses Alibaba of running large "distillation" campaigns against it, saying it deployed around 25,000 fake accounts to train its own models on Claude.

items.title: Over one million undocumented migrants apply for residency in Spain
items.description: The Socialist government's mass regularisation programme, which closed on 30 June, comes as others European countries crack down on irregular immigration.

items.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
items.description: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

items.title: DR Congo opens Ebola nurseries to protect children separated from infected parents
items.description: As the Democratic Republic of the Congo battles a worsening Ebola outbreak, authorities have established special nurseries to care for children whose parents are receiving treatment. The centres provide shelter, daily care and psychological support while helping reduce the risk of infection.

items.title: India’s P-8i maritime patrol aircraft in Hawaii for RIMPAC exercise
items.description: RIMPAC provides a unique training opportunity while fostering and sustaining cooperative relationships among participants critical to ensuring the safety of sea lanes and security on the world's oceans

items.title: Pope urges Europe to protect migrants in visit to island frontier
items.description: Pope Leo XIV has travelled to the Italian island of Lampedusa, one of Europe's main gateways for migrants crossing the Mediterranean. In a powerful appeal, the pontiff urged Europe to do more to protect and integrate those fleeing conflict and poverty.

items.title: Guinea court cuts ex-PM Fofana’s jail term in landmark corruption case
items.description: A Guinean appeals court has reduced former Prime Minister Ibrahima Kassory Fofana's prison sentence for corruption-related offences, paving the way for his release after more than four years in detention as the country's military-led government presses ahead with its anti-graft campaign.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
items.description: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

items.title: Palestinians rally outside Al-Shifa hospital, demanding medical evacuation
items.description: Protest outside Al-Shifa Medical Complex demanding Israel lift travel restrictions on those leaving the Gaza Strip.

items.title: The downfall: Julian Nagelsmann out as Germany coach
items.description: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Haitian medical students demand relocation of teaching hospital in capital
items.description: The institution in gang-controlled downtown Port-au-Prince plays a vital role in clinical training for young doctors.

items.title: Algeria out of World Cup after 2-0 loss to Switzerland
items.description: Switzerland is through to the World Cup Round of 16 after a 2-0 win over Algeria in Vancouver.

items.title: Santilli sworn in as Cabinet chief as Milei reshuffles pack
items.description: Diego Santilli formally took over as President Javier Milei's Cabinet chief on Tuesday, completing a transition triggered by Manuel Adorni's resignation amid a corruption investigation. Leer más

items.title: First Nations commissioner warns against Queensland adoption push
items.description: Australia's Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children commissioner has warned Queensland's push to increase adoptions could disproportionately affect First Nations children and risk repeating the harms of past forced child removals.

items.title: Chinese underground church figure Jin Mingri freed from prison
items.description: The Zion Church founder's release comes after a direct plea to Xi Jinping from Donald Trump.

items.title: More Chinese investment good for wider relationship, says India’s envoy to China
items.description: Noting that trade is expanding but lopsided, Ambassador Vikram Doraiswami pitches for more Indian exports to China, especially in areas such as pharmaceuticals where India is globally competitive

items.title: Trial for Bundibugyo Ebola treatment starts in DRC, WHO says
items.description: The trial ‌will evaluate Mapp Biopharmaceutical's experimental MBP134 antibody as a standalone treatment for Bundibugyo Ebola, as well as in combination with Gilead Sciences' antiviral drug remdesivir, the WHO said

items.title: Milei-linked Fundación Faro told to name donors behind US$4.8mn windfall
items.description: Fundación Faro presented itself as a platform of ideas. But the libertarian think tank’s opaque finances, links to key Milei insiders and 400-fold surge in donations have left the organisation facing uncomfortable questions from regulators and judges. Leer más

items.title: Egypt makes history, heartbreak for Cape Verde at World Cup
items.description: Heartbreak for Cape Verde as the World Cup debutants bowed out after a thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina. The Blue Sharks twice battled back to level the match, pushing Lionel Messi's side to the limit in one of the tournament's biggest surprise performances.

items.title: Meet Mr Row Row, the man who invented Norway's viral World Cup Viking row
items.description: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

items.title: Cabinets and wardrobes
items.description: Appointment of Diego Santilli places the relations of the ruling La Libertad Avanza party with its chief ally on a new footing. Leer más

items.title: Messi returns 'home' to lead Argentina World Cup charge in Miami
items.description: Argentina's skipper returns to the Florida city he now calls home for a last 32 clash against the surprise package of the tournament: Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: United States at 250: More prosperous, more diverse, yet more divided
items.description: Demographic change and economic growth have reshaped America over the past 50 years; Immigrants remain the strongest believers in American Dream. Leer más

items.title: Moment of destiny for France's Le Pen in verdict to decide her future in presidential race
items.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing against a verdict which found her guilty of misusing EU funds.

items.title: Algeria counts votes in legislative poll marked by low turnout
items.description: The electoral board announced on Thursday night a provisional 20-percent turnout. If confirmed, it would account for a record low in comparison to 23 percent in 2021

items.title: Venezuela FM visits Indian field hospital; expresses gratitude for earthquake relief work
items.description: Working alongside Venezuelan authorities, Indian rescue and medical teams are providing emergency medical care and conducting search and rescue missions, the Indian embassy said

items.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
items.description: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

items.title: Belgian coach says his comments were not a criticism of African football
items.description: Rudi Garcia said he was referring to teams unused to dealing with the kind of pressure that comes with managing a lead in high-level World Cup game

items.title: AFP to push Australia's policing model to UN
items.description: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

items.title: Trump says Netanyahu 'knows who the boss is', says sources
items.description: "We get along very well. [Mr. Netanyahu] knows who the boss is," Mr. Trump said in a brief phone interview with Axios, referring to himself

items.title: Fake agency scandal: PDP faction demands Gbajabiamila ouster, independent probe
items.description: The faction states that the scandal has brought embarrassment to the country. The post Fake agency scandal: PDP faction demands Gbajabiamila ouster, independent probe appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Milei plans to attend inaugurations of new Peru and Colombia presidents
items.description: President will travel to Lima and Bogotá for inauguration ceremonies; Milei welcomes election victories of Keiko Fujimori and Abelardo de la Espriella as signs of a broader right-ward shift in Latin America. Leer más

items.title: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
items.description: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

items.title: What did SCOTUS rule on birthright citizenship? | Explained
items.description: Why and how did U.S. President Donald Trump seek to limit birthright citizenship and what did the Supreme Court rule? Has this always been a political issue in the U.S.? What is the link between birthright citizenship and slavery? How has the judgment affected MAGA supporters?

items.title: Marcela Pagano: ‘It’s become evident that it is Karina Milei who calls the shots’
items.description: National deputy says Javier Milei’s government "has covered up Manuel Adorni’s corruption," that voters have fallen out of love with La Libertad Avanza and that Karina Milei, the President’s sister, is the one who’s really in charge in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Turkey steps up arrests ahead of NATO summit
items.description: Journalists, academics and members of left-wing groups have reportedly been detained during police raids in several Turkish provinces. Turkey's capital Ankara is set to host a major NATO summit this week.
